goal: Return a compiled JITKernel from a parameterized function.
ordered_steps: Decorate outer Python factory with @tilelang.jit or @tilelang.jit(out_idx=[...]).
Define an inner @T.prim_func with T.Tensor parameter annotations.
Inside the prim_func, use with T.Kernel(...) to define block-level work.
Return the prim_func from the outer factory.
Call the factory with sizes/config values to get a JITKernel.
minimal_example_summary: examples/gemm/example_gemm.py defines matmul(...) returning an inner gemm PrimFunc; matmul(...) call returns a kernel object.
input_output_notes: With out_idx=[-1], output tensors may be allocated/returned by adapter when invoking the kernel.
prerequisites: TileLang runtime built; target/backend available; tensor shapes match annotations.
failure_modes: Using out_idx in eager mode raises ValueError.
Calling T.Kernel outside @tilelang.jit/@T.prim_func raises JITNoBuilderError.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve target string from vendor/model before compiling.
Check pattern/device_strategy for allowed intrinsics and memory layout assumptions.
Create runtime tensors on the same device/backend as the target.
Choose profiler/benchmark backend compatible with the target.

NVIDIA CUDA: Use target="cuda" or exact sm target.
CUDA profiler backends such as event/cupti/cudagraph apply only on CUDA.
For WGMMA/TCGEN/TMA, require exact architecture and explicit synchronization rules.
AMD HIP: Prefer HIP/AMD examples for attention/GEMM when available.
CPU: Use target="llvm" or "c".
Use T.Kernel(..., is_cpu=True) where CPU examples do so.
Do not use GPU-only async/tensor-core/barrier APIs.
Metal/WebGPU: Use target="metal" or "webgpu" only with generic patterns unless tests prove support.
Validate generated code before assuming performance.

scenario: Compile, run, validate, inspect source, benchmark
goal: Exercise a TileLang kernel end to end with PyTorch tensors.
ordered_steps: Instantiate the JIT factory to get a kernel.
Create CUDA/HIP/CPU tensors matching T.Tensor shapes and dtypes.
Call kernel(*inputs) or kernel(inputs, output) depending on out_idx.
Compare against reference with torch.testing.assert_close.
Inspect generated code with kernel.get_kernel_source().
Benchmark with kernel.get_profiler().do_bench().
minimal_example_summary: quickstart.py runs matmul_relu_kernel(a,b,c), validates against torch.relu(a @ b), prints generated CUDA, and benchmarks latency.
input_output_notes: Input/output convention depends on out_idx and explicit output tensors.
prerequisites: PyTorch installed; device target matches tensors; numeric tolerances chosen for dtype.
failure_modes: Shape/dtype mismatch in host checks.
Backend unavailable or CUDA source compile failure.
Tolerance too strict for fp16/fp8 accumulation.

Use T.Kernel(..., is_cpu=True) where CPU examples do so.
Do not use GPU-only async/tensor-core/barrier APIs.
Metal/WebGPU: Use target="metal" or "webgpu" only with generic patterns unless tests prove support.

goal: Implement tiled matrix multiplication body.
ordered_steps: Open T.Kernel with grid ceildiv(N, block_N), ceildiv(M, block_M).
Allocate A_shared/B_shared in shared memory and C_local in local.fragment.
T.clear(C_local).
For each K tile in T.Pipelined(..., num_stages=N), copy A/B global tiles into shared.
Call T.gemm(A_shared, B_shared, C_local).
Optionally run epilogue over C_local with T.Parallel.
T.copy(C_local, C[...]) to write back.
minimal_example_summary: Basic GEMM and quickstart show the canonical operator body.
input_output_notes: A/B shapes and transpose flags must align with C; C must be 2D for T.gemm.
prerequisites: block sizes compatible with architecture and operand shapes.
failure_modes: T.gemm shape assertions fail if M/K/N mismatch.
Pipeline num_stages=0 disables pipeline.
Incorrect C dtype or fragment scope can lower poorly.

goal: Move tiles between global/shared/local/fragment scopes.
ordered_steps: Allocate destination buffer in desired scope.
Call T.copy(src_region, dst_buffer_or_region).
Use coalesced_width/eviction_policy/disable_tma annotations only when needed.
Use T.async_copy for explicit async cp.async path and manage waits separately.
Use T.tma_copy with barrier for user-managed TMA loads/stores.
minimal_example_summary: copy_op.py defines synchronous copy plus explicit async and TMA variants.
input_output_notes: copy extents are inferred from Buffer/BufferRegion/BufferLoad; scalar BufferLoad to BufferLoad lowers to a direct store.
prerequisites: Source/destination regions must expose extents or shape.
failure_modes: Both sides without extents raise assertion.
Extent mismatch/broadcast-like usage is limited.
T.async_copy does not auto-insert wait.
T.tma_copy load requires barrier.

goal: Reduce shared/fragment data along a dimension.
ordered_steps: Allocate input/output buffers in shared or fragment scope.
Ensure output shape equals input with reduced dim removed or kept as size 1.
Call T.reduce_sum/max/min/... with dim and clear flag.
Copy reduced result to destination if needed.
minimal_example_summary: reduce_op.py implements reductions as macros that stage shared buffers through fragments when needed.
input_output_notes: Output shape must be [X,Y] or [X,1,Y] for reducing input [X,d,Y].
prerequisites: Input and output must be shared or fragment buffers.
failure_modes: Invalid output shape raises ValueError.
Invalid buffer scopes raise ValueError.
nan_propagate applies only to selected max/min half-like CUDA paths.

goal: Control or override inferred layouts for buffers and parallel loops.
ordered_steps: Create a T.Layout or T.Fragment mapping.
Pass loop_layout to T.Parallel/T.copy or call T.annotate_layout({buffer: layout}).
Let LayoutInference validate/infer missing layouts.
Inspect transform/layout tests when validation fails.
minimal_example_summary: language/loop.py documents that outermost parallel loop carries layout annotation and InputDim must match nest depth.
input_output_notes: Layout maps buffer data vars or parallel loop iteration space to physical layout.
prerequisites: Layout object must match buffer/loop dimensionality.
failure_modes: Mismatched InputDim or inner-loop annotation causes compilation error.
Fragment buffers require Fragment layout, not generic callable.

goal: Search block sizes, thread count, and pipeline stages for a kernel factory.
ordered_steps: Define a config list/dict of tunable parameters.
Decorate kernel factory with @tilelang.autotune(configs=...).
Stack @tilelang.jit on the function as shown in docs/examples.
Run tuning with representative inputs or constraints.
Use selected config for final kernel/benchmark.
minimal_example_summary: autotuning docs show @tilelang.autotune above @tilelang.jit and expose block sizes as parameters.
input_output_notes: Autotune changes compile-time parameters; input/output tensors still follow kernel annotations.
prerequisites: Representative input tensors and a bounded config space.
failure_modes: Large config spaces cause long compile time.
Invalid configs can fail compilation unless ignored by tuner.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known

Run autotune on the final target device/model; do not reuse timings across vendors.

goal: Trace generated TIR and source when an operator fails.
ordered_steps: Set verbose/debug_root_path or call get_kernel_source/script where available.
Check failing primitive against docs and source_chunks.
Inspect transform pass tests for the relevant pass.
If failure is in T.copy/T.gemm/layout, inspect LowerTileOp/LayoutInference source chunks.
minimal_example_summary: debug docs describe progressive lowering where T.copy expands to T.Parallel then lower-level statements.
input_output_notes: Debug artifacts are host-side generated source/TIR; not an operator output.
prerequisites: Compilation reaches enough stages to emit source or TIR.
failure_modes: Hardware-specific lowering can fail only after target detection.
Dynamic shape or layout inference failures may require source fallback.

goal: Compile a small TileLang GEMM for CPU/C target path.
ordered_steps: Decorate with @tilelang.jit(target="c") or call compile with CPU target.
Use with T.Kernel(..., is_cpu=True) to avoid GPU thread bindings.
Use local buffers and T.gemm scalar path.
Run tests on CPU arrays/tensors.
minimal_example_summary: CPU GEMM tests verify T.gemm works with target="c" and is_cpu=True kernel frames.
input_output_notes: CPU target is useful for codegen validation; performance model differs from GPU.
prerequisites: CPU backend built and supported.
failure_modes: GPU-only primitives like WGMMA/TMA are not applicable.
Thread bindings are skipped for is_cpu=True.

goal: Unpack/dequantize quantized operands inside the K pipeline before T.gemm.
ordered_steps: Define helper to unpack low-bit storage to compute dtype.
Allocate shared packed B and fragment/local dequantized B.
Copy packed B to shared, then local/fragment.
Use T.Parallel to unpack/dequantize into a dense tile.
Call T.gemm on dense/dequantized tile and activation tile.
Copy accumulator to output, respecting orientation.
minimal_example_summary: example_dequant_gemm_w4a8.py unpacks uint8-packed int4 values into int8 fragments and multiplies them with A using T.gemm.
input_output_notes: Packed operand shapes divide K by elems-per-byte; output may be transposed as Ct[N,M].
prerequisites: K and block_K compatible with packing factor.
Correct signed reinterpret/dequant logic for quant format.
failure_modes: Wrong unpack position or signed extension corrupts values.
Mismatched output orientation breaks reference comparison.
Swizzled layout annotations must match buffer shape.

goal: Compute multiple independent GEMMs packed into one launch using group size/offset metadata.
ordered_steps: Precompute batch_sizes, batch_offsets, and batch_padded_offsets on host.
Pass offset tensors to the JIT kernel.
Map CTA M-block id to current group with padded offsets.
Compute actual_rows for tail guard.
Run normal tiled GEMM for the current group.
Write only rows with i < actual_rows.
minimal_example_summary: example_grouped_gemm_fwd.py packs variable group row counts, computes current group inside the kernel, and guards output writes.
input_output_notes: A is concatenated across groups; B has group dimension; offsets are int32 tensors.
prerequisites: Host offset construction must match block_M padding.
batch_sum equals sum(batch_sizes).
failure_modes: Incorrect padded offsets map CTAs to wrong group.
Tail rows must be guarded.
TensorSupplyType.Auto may be needed for profiling with metadata tensors.

goal: Compute per-row inverse RMS and apply it to each element.
ordered_steps: Copy row tile to shared/local buffers.
Square elements in T.Parallel.
Use T.reduce_sum over feature dimension.
Apply T.rsqrt(sum / N + eps).
Scale original values by per-row inverse RMS.
Copy normalized tile to output.
minimal_example_summary: examples/norm/rms_norm.py contains both full-row and split-K RMSNorm variants.
input_output_notes: Output shape matches input; split-K version loops over column tiles twice.
prerequisites: Feature dimension and block size fit chosen memory strategy.
failure_modes: Full-row allocation can be too large when N is large.
Reduction dim/output shape must match reduce_op contract.

goal: Compile a kernel whose M/N/K are runtime symbols.
ordered_steps: Declare symbols with T.dynamic("name").
Use symbols in T.Tensor shapes and T.ceildiv grid expressions.
Keep block sizes and tuning knobs as compile-time parameters.
Instantiate kernel once with static knobs.
Run with tensors carrying concrete runtime shapes.
Benchmark with profiler.do_bench(input_tensors=[...]) when dynamic tensors are needed.
minimal_example_summary: examples/dynamic_shape/example_dynamic.py declares M/N/K as dynamic symbols and profiles with concrete input tensors.
input_output_notes: Tensor shapes must be consistent with runtime symbolic values inferred from inputs.
prerequisites: Backend/profiler must support dynamic symbolic constraints for the scenario.
failure_modes: Missing input_tensors can prevent profiler from resolving dynamic sizes.
Transposed shapes must match trans_A/trans_B flags.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve target string from vendor/model before compiling.
Check pattern/device_strategy for allowed intrinsics and memory layout assumptions.
Create runtime tensors on the same device/backend as the target.
Choose profiler/benchmark backend compatible with the target.
For dynamic shapes, pass concrete input_tensors to profiler on the target device.

goal: Use explicit async tensor-core primitives without implicit waits.
ordered_steps: Stage operands into shared memory or required scopes.
Issue T.wgmma_gemm or T.tcgen05_gemm with clear_accum as needed.
For WGMMA, call T.wait_wgmma before reading accumulator.
For TCGEN05, allocate/pass mbarrier and call the required mbarrier wait path.
Copy accumulator to output only after explicit synchronization.
minimal_example_summary: test_tilelang_language_wgmma_gemm.py checks that explicit WGMMA emits no implicit wait and requires a user wait.
input_output_notes: Architecture-specific; Hopper WGMMA and Blackwell TCGEN05 have different requirements.
prerequisites: CUDA compute capability and target must support the selected instruction path.
failure_modes: Explicit WGMMA rejects MMA fallback.
Missing wait/barrier can read incomplete accumulators.
TCGEN05 2CTA requires matching cluster_dims.

Metal/WebGPU: Use target="metal" or "webgpu" only with generic patterns unless tests prove support.

limitations: Architecture-specific: Hopper WGMMA and Blackwell TCGEN05 are not portable fallbacks.

scenario: Pass configuration and compiler behavior control
goal: Adjust lowering/codegen knobs when operator generation hits backend or pass behavior issues.
ordered_steps: Inspect PassConfigKey in apis.jsonl/source_chunks.
Pass pass_configs to @tilelang.jit or tilelang.compile.
Use narrow flags such as disabling TMA/WGMMA/vectorization only for diagnosis or known constraints.
Re-run source inspection and tests after changing pass config.
minimal_example_summary: pass_config.py enumerates keys for async copy, WGMMA, TMA lowering, vectorization, layout visualization, storage rewrite, and debug output.
input_output_notes: Pass configs affect compilation, not kernel ABI.
prerequisites: Know which lowering path is failing or being tuned.
failure_modes: Disabling passes can hide performance issues or change semantics.
Some flags are deprecated or backend-specific.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve target string from vendor/model before compiling.
Check pattern/device_strategy for allowed intrinsics and memory layout assumptions.
Create runtime tensors on the same device/backend as the target.

Metal/WebGPU: Use target="metal" or "webgpu" only with generic patterns unless tests prove support.

goal: Compile and validate a GEMM that partitions K and atomically accumulates partial tiles.
ordered_steps: Choose split_k and tile sizes that expose more CTAs without excessive atomic pressure.
Initialize or clear the output tensor before partial accumulation.
Map the third grid dimension or program interval to the K partition.
Use the normal GEMM mainloop over the partition-local K range.
Accumulate partial results with atomic add or a vectorized atomic helper.
Compare against dense A @ B before benchmarking.
minimal_example_summary: Split-K examples add K partitions and atomic writeback around the standard tiled GEMM body.
input_output_notes: Output accumulation semantics differ from normal GEMM; initialization and accumulator dtype are part of correctness.
prerequisites: Large enough K or irregular occupancy to justify split-K.
Backend supports the required atomic accumulation path.
failure_modes: Output not initialized before atomics.
Too many split partitions increase atomic contention.
Accumulator dtype or vectorized atomic path does not match target support.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Verify atomic support and dtype behavior on the selected backend.
Benchmark against non-split GEMM before keeping the extra scheduling complexity.

goal: Validate sparse metadata layout, active block selection, and sparse tensor-core or SIMT GEMM behavior.
ordered_steps: Identify the exact sparse metadata ABI before writing TileLang code.
Stage compressed operands or active dense blocks according to the selected pattern.
Use T.gemm_sp/T.gemm_sp_v2 only when metadata layout and target support are confirmed.
Provide a dense reference path for correctness comparison.
Benchmark only after metadata and active-block coverage are validated.
minimal_example_summary: Sparse tensor-core and block-sparse examples pair operand staging with metadata-driven sparse computation.
input_output_notes: Sparse metadata tensors are part of the operator ABI and cannot be inferred from shapes alone.
prerequisites: Known sparsity format and target support for the selected sparse path.
failure_modes: Metadata packing does not match the kernel interpretation.
Sparse tensor-core path is used on an unsupported target.
Dense reference comparison omits inactive blocks or padding.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Confirm sparse tensor-core or SIMT fallback support for the resolved target.
Do not reuse NVIDIA sparse metadata assumptions on other backends without source evidence.

goal: Validate tiled QK/PV attention with online softmax state and no full attention matrix materialization.
ordered_steps: Confirm Q/K/V layout, head dimension, mask or causal semantics, and output tensors.
Stage Q/K/V tiles into shared or fragments following the selected source pattern.
Maintain row-wise max and sum state in the same order as the reference algorithm.
Compute PV accumulation after numerically stable score normalization.
Compare against PyTorch or model-runtime reference with dtype-appropriate tolerance.
minimal_example_summary: Flash attention examples combine tiled QK, online softmax statistics, and PV accumulation.
input_output_notes: Layout, scale, mask, and optional logsumexp/stat tensors are part of the operator contract.
prerequisites: Reference implementation and exact tensor layout are available.
failure_modes: Changing online softmax update order breaks numerical correctness.
Mask or layout semantics are copied from a nonmatching example.
Target-specific layout annotations are omitted.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve backend before choosing layout annotations and tensor-core path.
Treat WGMMA/TMA variants as source-specific, not generic flash attention defaults.

goal: Validate attention decode kernels that read KV cache pages or sequence metadata.
ordered_steps: Identify page/block table tensors, sequence lengths, and active token metadata.
Map grid dimensions to batch, head, page, or token blocks exactly as the source pattern requires.
Compute QK scores and reductions over active cache blocks only.
Apply weighted V accumulation and write output layout expected by the runtime.
Test with edge cases: short sequence, page boundary, and padded block.
minimal_example_summary: Decode examples combine KV-cache metadata with tiled attention score and value accumulation.
input_output_notes: Paged metadata and variable sequence lengths are part of the ABI.
prerequisites: Runtime KV-cache layout and reference decode output are known.
failure_modes: Page metadata is interpreted with the wrong stride or dtype.
Padded tokens contribute to score reductions.
Output layout does not match runtime expectation.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve target before using persistent, TMA, or warp-specialized decode variants.
Prefer generic decode first when backend evidence is weak.

goal: Validate a convolution pattern that maps filter windows to tiled GEMM-like computation and then tune its block sizes.
ordered_steps: Confirm input layout, filter layout, stride, padding, dilation, and output shape formula.
Start from the nearest convolution example before adding autotune.
Validate boundary masks for padded or non-divisible spatial tiles.
Build a small config space for block sizes and pipeline stages.
Compare against framework convolution reference before benchmarking.
minimal_example_summary: Convolution examples show direct and autotuned convolution workflows around GEMM-like tiling.
input_output_notes: Layout and padding semantics must match the framework reference exactly.
prerequisites: Known convolution ABI and representative shapes for tuning.
failure_modes: Wrong layout conversion silently produces incorrect output.
Boundary or padding guards are missing.
Autotune space includes invalid block/filter combinations.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve target before choosing tensor-core assumptions or autotune bounds.
Keep a small validation-first config space on unfamiliar backends.

goal: Validate a simple T.Parallel tile kernel or fused epilogue before adding target-specific vectorization.
ordered_steps: Map linear or 2-D element space to T.Kernel grid and T.Parallel tile loops.
Guard out-of-bound elements when total size is not divisible by tile size.
Implement the scalar operation or epilogue in the innermost parallel loop.
Compare against a framework reference over aligned and tail sizes.
Add vectorization/coalescing hints only after correctness passes.
minimal_example_summary: Elementwise examples use T.Parallel for per-element operations and are a portable starting point for fusion.
input_output_notes: Output shape normally matches input shape unless the fusion contract says otherwise.
prerequisites: Operation semantics and dtype promotion rules are known.
failure_modes: Tail elements are not guarded.
Vectorization assumes alignment that inputs do not provide.
Host-side broadcasting semantics are not represented in the kernel.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Elementwise patterns are more portable, but still validate generated code on the selected backend.
Avoid tensor-core or CUDA-only assumptions unless the fused body introduces them.

goal: Validate per-row or per-block candidate selection, index handling, and output ordering for top-k kernels.
ordered_steps: Define K, row count, value dtype, index dtype, and tie/order semantics.
Stage candidate values and indices into local or shared buffers.
Update local top-k state while scanning the selected axis.
Write values and indices in the expected order.
Compare against torch.topk or model reference for representative rows and edge cases.
minimal_example_summary: Top-k examples combine candidate scanning, local/shared state, and value/index output tensors.
input_output_notes: Index dtype and tie ordering must match the caller's contract.
prerequisites: K is bounded enough for the chosen local/shared storage strategy.
failure_modes: Index output does not correspond to selected value after swaps.
Ties or NaNs differ from reference semantics.
K exceeds local buffer assumptions.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Validate selection logic before adding target-specific parallel reductions.
Benchmark against framework top-k only after value/index correctness is stable.

goal: Validate grouped/routed expert GEMM metadata, activation behavior, and output layout for MoE kernels.
ordered_steps: Confirm token routing metadata: group sizes, offsets, padded offsets, and expert weight layout.
Validate each GEMM stage independently when possible: shared expert, routed up/gate, activation/product, and down projection.
Guard actual rows for tail groups exactly as source patterns do.
Compare against a model-runtime or PyTorch reference over multiple routing distributions.
Benchmark only after ABI and activation numerical behavior match.
minimal_example_summary: Fused MoE examples combine grouped GEMM metadata with activation and multi-stage expert projections.
input_output_notes: MoE metadata tensors are application ABI, not optional tuning inputs.
prerequisites: Reference implementation and exact expert routing metadata are available.
failure_modes: Padded offsets map CTAs to the wrong expert group.
Activation approximation differs from model reference.
Pass configs are treated as incidental and then removed incorrectly.

device_profile_required: target string or arch if known
ordered_device_steps: Resolve backend before carrying over warp-specialization or pass config behavior.
Treat MoE ABI validation as higher priority than performance tuning.
